title: tetragonia glauca
intro: Tetragonia glauca, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi in Māori, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia, used as a leafy green vegetable and valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Tetragonia glauca include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. Tetragonia glauca can be prepared as a herbal remedy through drying, decoction, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
